subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV 2025, EC, Eurostat, OECD, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem, NATO) + Romanian official (INS, National Bank of Romania) + Romanian/regional press (Romania-Insider, Recorder, Balkan Insight, OSW).

Politics: a 2024 presidential election annulled over foreign-interference claims, re-run in 2025 (a centrist beat a far-right candidate), with the pro-EU coalition government toppled in a no-confidence vote on 2026-05-05 - acute turbulence the system absorbed.

Standout drags: the EU's largest deficit under austerity, stubbornly high inflation, steep demographic decline, war-adjacency, and underinsured seismic risk; strong alliance value and sound banks offset. Source-lean: MODERATE-HIGH Anglophone.

economic.notes: A credible central bank and sound banks, against the EU's largest deficit and stubbornly high inflation.

fiscal_state.notes: The EU's largest deficit (~7.7% GDP) under an excessive-deficit procedure, with a ~5%-of-GDP 2025 consolidation and ratings at risk; debt rising from a low base. (IMF; EC; OSW)

monetary_independence.notes: The BNR is reasonably credible (rate ~6.5%), tightly floating the leu; FX defence constrains independence. (BNR)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The leu (an EU non-eurozone currency) is not a reserve asset. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Among the EU's highest (~10.7%), driven by energy liberalisation and VAT, with the central bank revising its forecast up - slow, uncertain disinflation. (BNR; INS)

banking_stability.notes: Well-capitalised with moderate NPLs, though falling profits and sovereign-deficit linkages are flagged. (BNR FSR)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Autos and IT exports, EU-integrated nearshoring, with Black Sea gas (first flow ~2027) ahead and no resource curse; the auto sector softening. (EC; Romania-Insider)

institutional.notes: Credible statistics and a free press, against rule-of-law backsliding pressure and uneven capacity.

rule_of_law.notes: Improving from a corruption-heavy base, with functional anti-graft bodies under threat (statute-of-limitations rulings voiding convictions) and a 2025 judiciary scandal; corruption perception stagnant. (EC Rule of Law 2025; TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: INS is Eurostat-compliant and credible. (INS; Eurostat)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Improving but bureaucratic, with uneven EU-fund absorption. (OECD)

press_freedom.notes: Free and pluralistic (RSF ~49th) but with opaque political financing and ownership pressure. (RSF 2026)

political_social.notes: Strong liberties and EU/Schengen membership, against acute political turbulence and steep demographic decline.

political_stability.notes: A 2024 presidential election annulled over foreign-interference claims, then re-run in 2025 (a centrist beat a far-right candidate), with the pro-EU coalition government toppled in a no-confidence vote in May 2026 - acute turbulence, though courts and the ballot held. (Atlantic Council; Al Jazeera)

civil_liberties.notes: Strong, with robust protest and assembly rights. (EC; Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: An EU member in full Schengen since 2025 with lawful routes; a net diaspora-outflow country with modest inflows. (EC; INS)

social_cohesion.notes: A deep anti-establishment/far-right surge, an urban-rural split, and a huge diaspora. (Balkan Insight)

demographics.notes: A steep decline - emigration plus low fertility, with the population down sharply over decades - a structural long-term drag. (INS; Eurostat)

geopolitical.notes: Top-tier NATO Black Sea value, squarely offset by frontline-adjacent war exposure.

alliance_reliability.notes: A NATO Black Sea anchor (hosting a major air base, defence rising toward 3% of GDP) with deep US/EU ties. (NATO; Chatham House)

conflict_involvement.notes: Frontline-adjacent to the Russia-Ukraine war - repeated drone debris on its territory and Black Sea mine/hybrid risk - a genuine elevated tail. (Atlantic Council; War on the Rocks)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An EU regime with an open capital account. (EU)

physical_practical.notes: Improving infrastructure and low violent crime, against an underfunded health system and underinsured seismic risk.

climate.notes: SE-Europe heat and intensifying drought on large rain-fed agriculture, plus Danube flood swings, moderate readiness. (ND-GAIN; EEA)

healthcare.notes: Universal but underfunded, with heavy doctor emigration and rural gaps. (OECD Health 2025; WHO)

infrastructure.notes: EU-fund upgrades (motorways, rail) on an improving trajectory from a historically poor base. (EC; JASPERS)

crime_safety.notes: Low violent crime, with a major exception in road-fatality rates. (OSAC; EC Road Safety)

disaster_insurance.notes: A high-seismic capital (the Vrancea zone), with a mandatory catastrophe scheme but low (~20%) uptake - a large protection gap. (PAID; World Bank GFDRR)

summary: An EU-integrated, NATO-frontline economy with sound banks, a credible central bank, an improving (if pressured) rule-of-law trajectory, and high alliance value as a Black Sea anchor. These are heavily offset by the EU's largest deficit under an austerity package, stubbornly high inflation, an annulled-then-rerun presidential election followed by a government toppled in a May-2026 confidence vote, a far-right surge, a steep demographic decline, war-adjacency with drone debris on its territory, and underinsured seismic risk in a high-hazard capital.
